faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Floral Park home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, silken webbing in closet corners, tiny cream-colored larvae on fabric surfaces, and adult moths flying near wardrobes or windows. You may also notice shed larval casings or a musty odor in storage areas. Our Floral Park technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm infestations and identify the moth species.

faqs.question: What factors affect the cost of clothes moth control in Floral Park?
faqs.answer: Pricing between $550 and $5,000 depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, the number of affected rooms or closets, and whether treatment requires specialized applications for delicate fabrics or difficult-to-reach areas. Larger Floral Park homes with extensive textile collections or multiple infested areas will require more comprehensive treatment plans, while single-room infestations are typically at the lower end of the range.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a clothes moth problem in Floral Park?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Floral Park residents whenever possible. Because clothes moths can cause significant damage to expensive clothing and textiles quickly, we prioritize rapid response to protect your belongings. Our team will schedule an inspection at your convenience and begin treatment immediately upon confirmation of the infestation.

faqs.question: What does your clothes moth treatment process include for Floral Park homes?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive treatment includes a detailed inspection of closets, storage areas, and upholstered furniture, targeted application of safe insecticides to affected areas, pheromone trapping systems to monitor activity, steam treatment or heat remediation when appropriate, and guidance on proper fabric storage and prevention measures. We also provide follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination and offer ongoing protection plans for Floral Park homes with valuable textile collections.
